event_claims.description: CSC/Pamplona confirmed it was willing to increase its all-cash purchase price to $21.25 per share as a last and best offer, conditioned on two weeks of exclusive negotiations.
event_claims.quote_text: Later that same day, the representative of CSC/Pamplona called the members of the Transaction Committee to confirm that CSC/Pamplona were willing to increase their all-cash purchase price to $21.25 per share as a last and best offer, provided that Mac-Gray agree to a two week period of exclusive negotiations.

event_claims.description: The Special Committee authorized Mac-Gray to enter into an exclusivity agreement for exclusive negotiations until October 12, 2013 on the terms of the CSC/Pamplona final proposal.
event_claims.quote_text: After discussion, the Special Committee authorized Mac-Gray to enter into an exclusivity agreement providing for exclusive negotiations until 5 p.m. ET on October 12, 2013 on the terms included in the CSC/Pamplona final proposal

event_claims.description: CSC, Pamplona and Mac-Gray executed an exclusivity agreement providing for exclusive negotiations until October 12, 2013.
event_claims.quote_text: On September 24, 2013, CSC, Pamplona and Mac-Gray executed the exclusivity agreement providing for exclusive negotiations until 5 p.m. ET on October 12, 2013

event_claims.description: Moab determined it would not roll over any Moab shares in the transaction.
event_claims.quote_text: As a result of such discussions, it was determined that Moab would not rollover any Moab shares in the transaction.

event_claims.description: Pamplona committed to fund 100% of the capital needed for closing.
event_claims.quote_text: Pamplona was committing to fund 100% of the capital needed for closing

participation_count_claims.quote_text: The Special Committee noted that the indication of interest submitted by Party B, including the Party B options, was on its face higher than the CSC/Pamplona proposal but further discussed the risks and complexities involved with measuring and achieving the performance required to vest the Party B options. The Special Committee also noted that Party B would likely be financing the transaction with a combination of equity from affiliated funds and third party debt capital, which would involve more risk than the Pamplona-funded CSC/Pamplona proposal, which did not require any third party financing. Following a discussion, the Special Committee concluded that the revised indication of interest submitted by CSC/Pamplona had the potential of delivering the most value to stockholders, provided greater certainty and involved less potential risk than the other indications.
